description: Lock Craft Quality Marks to 13th-century institutional craft supervision and hallmarking, separate from earlier mill-production networks.

checks.reason: The node represents a 13th-century source window from London goldsmith craft supervision in 1238 to statutory hallmarking in 1300.
The source assigns quality supervision and assay marking to organized guardians of the goldsmith craft.
Hallmarking depends on legal fineness standards, assay testing, and component marks for measured purity and provenance.
The 1000-century sawmill network node is mechanical and logistical, not dependent on later institutional hallmark or craft-inspection marks.
The 1000-century water-powered oil-press node depends on watermills and crop inputs, not later institutional craft marks.
Guild certification seals remain downstream of the institutional quality-mark and craft-supervision lineage.
Paper watermarks remain a later authentication-mark system that inherits the institutional marking concept.
